completeness.note: kind:infra — verification is workflow-lint + advisory-semantics assertion, not unit tests

addressed_inline.summary: GitHub event context interpolated directly into the shell via ${{ }} (event_name, pull_request.base.sha, github.event.before). Values are GitHub-controlled SHA/enum (not attacker-controlled free text), so not exploitable — but drifts from the repo convention (release-on-merge.yml passes context via env: then references $VAR).
addressed_inline.resolution: inline fix: moved all three context values to env: (EVENT_NAME, PR_BASE_SHA, PUSH_BEFORE_SHA), referenced as shell vars. Re-review: zero ${{ }} expansion in any run body.

architecture.checks: timeout-minutes bounded (10), concurrency cancel-in-progress, actions/checkout@v4 pinned, no secrets, no unbounded external calls, no missing-timeout network calls

security.note: no plaintext secrets; event-context injection addressed inline via env-passing

quality.cr10_performance: no anti-pattern matches (no loops over collections beyond bounded 2-iter ref loop; no DB/RPC/FS)
quality.test_coverage: kind:infra — advisory-semantics assertion is the verification gate, hand-traced + scratch-run

carve_out: single-reader pass justified: 148-156 lines, 1 file (within CR-02 threshold of <=200 lines AND <=5 files)
coverage_gaps: shellcheck absent (mitigated: bash -n syntax check on each run-step body; repo contract is stdlib-only tooling, matching branch-ci.yml)
